name: yoyo-migrations

metadata.name: yoyo-migrations
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Yoyo database migrations

Yoyo-migrations is a database schema migration tool.
Database migrations can be written as SQL files or Python scripts.

## What does yoyo-migrations do?

As your database application evolves, changes to the database schema may be
required. Yoyo lets you write migration scripts in raw SQL or Python containing
SQL statements to migrate your database schema to a new version.

A simple migration script looks like this:

% code::python
%
% # file: migrations/0001.create-foo.py
% from yoyo import step
% step(
%     "CREATE TABLE foo (id INT, bar VARCHAR(20), PRIMARY KEY (id))",
%     "DROP TABLE foo",
% )

Yoyo manages these database migration scripts,
gives you command line tools to apply and rollback migrations,
and manages dependencies between migrations.

## Database support

PostgreSQL, MySQL and SQLite databases are supported.
ODBC and Oracle database backends are available (but unsupported).

## Documentation and code

[Yoyo migrations documentation](https://ollycope.com/software/yoyo/)
| [Repository and issue tracker](https://sr.ht/~olly/yoyo/)

modules.name: yoyo.internalmigrations
documentation: Migrate yoyo's internal table structure

functions.name: yoyo.internalmigrations.upgrade
documentation:
Check the currently installed yoyo migrations version and update the
internal schema

functions.name: yoyo.internalmigrations.get_current_version
documentation: Return the currently installed yoyo migrations schema version

functions.name: yoyo.internalmigrations.mark_schema_version
documentation: Mark the given version as having been applied

modules.name: yoyo.internalmigrations.v1
documentation:
Version 1 schema.

Compatible with yoyo-migrations < 6.0

modules.name: yoyo.internalmigrations.v2
documentation:
Version 2 schema.

Compatible with yoyo-migrations >=  6.0

classes.name: yoyo.topologicalsort.CycleError
documentation:
Raised when cycles exist in the input graph.

The second element in the args attribute of instances will contain the
sequence of nodes in which the cycle lies.

functions.name: yoyo.connections.get_backend
documentation:
Connect to the given DB uri in the format
`driver://user:pass@host:port/database_name?param=value`,
returning a {class}`DatabaseBackend` object

functions.name: yoyo.connections.parse_uri
documentation:
Examples:

```
>>> parse_uri('postgres://fred:bassett@server:5432/fredsdatabase')
('postgres', 'fred', 'bassett', 'server', 5432, 'fredsdatabase', None)
>>> parse_uri('mysql:///jimsdatabase')
('mysql', None, None, None, None, 'jimsdatabase', None, None)
>>> parse_uri('odbc://user:password@server/database?DSN=dsn')
('odbc', 'user', 'password', 'server', None, 'database', {'DSN':'dsn'})
```

classes.name: yoyo.connections.BadConnectionURI
documentation: An invalid connection URI

functions.name: yoyo.utils.getch

Read a single character without echoing to the console and without

functions.name: yoyo.utils.prompt
documentation: Display the given prompt and list of options and return the user selection.

functions.name: yoyo.utils.plural
documentation:
```pycon
>>> plural(1, '%d dead frog', '%d dead frogs')
'1 dead frog'
>>> plural(2, '%d dead frog', '%d dead frogs')
'2 dead frogs'
```

functions.name: yoyo.utils.get_editor
documentation: Return the user's preferred visual editor

functions.name: yoyo.utils.get_random_string
documentation: Return a random string of `length` characters

functions.name: yoyo.utils.change_param_style
documentation:
---
param bind_parameters: A dict of bind parameters for the query
param sql: An SQL str
param target_style: A DBAPI paramstyle value (eg 'qmark', 'format', etc)
return: |-
  tuple of `(sql, bind_parameters)`. `sql` will be rewritten with
  the target paramstyle; `bind_parameters` will be a tuple or
  dict as required.
---

functions.name: yoyo.utils.unidecode
documentation: Return `s` with unicode diacritics removed.

Backend for PostgreSQL and PostgreSQL compatible databases.

This backend uses psycopg2. See
{class}`yoyo.backends.core.postgresql.PostgresqlPsycopgBackend`
if you need psycopg3.

functions.name: yoyo.config.update_argparser_defaults
documentation:
Update an ArgumentParser's defaults.

Unlike ArgumentParser.set_defaults this will only set defaults for
arguments the parser has configured.

functions.name: yoyo.config.read_config
documentation:
Read the configuration file at `src` and construct a ConfigParser instance.

If `src` is None a new, empty ConfigParse object will be created.

functions.name: yoyo.config._make_path
documentation:
Return a fully resolved Path. Raises FileNotFoundError if the path does not
exist.

functions.name: yoyo.config.save_config
documentation: Write the configuration file to `path`.

functions.name: yoyo.config.find_config
documentation: Find the closest config file in the cwd or a parent directory

classes.name: yoyo.config.CircularReferenceError
documentation: %include or %inherit directive has created a circular reference.

functions.name: yoyo.backends.base.get_dbapi_module
documentation: Import and return the named DB-API driver module

classes.name: yoyo.backends.base.TransactionManager

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.TransactionManager._do_begin
documentation: Instruct the backend to begin a transaction

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.TransactionManager._do_commit
documentation: Instruct the backend to commit the transaction

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.TransactionManager._do_rollback
documentation: Instruct the backend to roll back the transaction

Returned by the {meth}`~yoyo.backends.DatabaseBackend.transaction`
context manager.

If rollback is called, the transaction is flagged to be rolled back
when the context manager block closes

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.SavepointTransactionManager._do_commit
documentation:
This does nothing.

Trying to the release savepoint here could cause an database error in
databases where DDL queries cause the transaction to be committed
and all savepoints released.

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend._load_driver_module
documentation: Load the dbapi driver module and register the base exception class

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.format_sql
documentation:
Take a string in the format used by the various `..._sql` class
variables and format it.

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.init_connection
documentation:
Called when creating a connection or after a rollback. May do any
db specific tasks required to make the connection ready for use.

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.copy
documentation:
Return a copy of the backend with a independent db
connection.

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend._check_transactional_ddl
documentation:
Return True if the database supports committing/rolling back
DDL statements within a transaction

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.list_tables
documentation:
Return a list of tables present in the backend.
This is used by the test suite to clean up tables
generated during testing

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.begin
documentation: Begin a new transaction

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.savepoint
documentation: Create a new savepoint with the given id

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.savepoint_release
documentation: Release (commit) the savepoint with the given id

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.savepoint_rollback
documentation: Rollback the savepoint with the given id

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.disable_transactions
documentation:
Disable the connection's transaction support, for example by
setting the isolation mode to 'autocommit'

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.lock
documentation:
Create a lock to prevent concurrent migrations.

```
param timeout

duration in seconds before raising a LockTimeout error.
```

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.execute
documentation:
Create a new cursor, execute a single statement and return the cursor
object.

```
param sql

A single SQL statement, optionally with named parameters
(eg 'SELECT * FROM foo WHERE :bar IS NULL')

param params

A dictionary of parameters
```

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.create_lock_table
documentation: Create the lock table if it does not already exist.

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.ensure_internal_schema_updated
documentation: Check and upgrade yoyo's internal schema.

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.get_applied_migration_hashes
documentation:
Return the list of migration hashes in the order in which they
were applied

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.to_apply
documentation: Return the subset of migrations not already applied.

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.to_rollback
documentation:
Return the subset of migrations already applied and which may be
rolled back.

The order of migrations will be reversed.

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.apply_migrations_only
documentation:
Apply the list of migrations, but do not run any post-apply hooks
present.

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.run_post_apply
documentation: Run any post-apply migrations present in ``migrations``

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.apply_one
documentation: Apply a single migration

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.rollback_one
documentation: Rollback a single migration

methods.name: yoyo.backends.base.DatabaseBackend.get_log_data
documentation: Return a dict of data for insertion into the `_yoyo_log` table

functions.name: yoyo.migrations._is_migration_file
documentation: Return True if the given path matches a migration file pattern

functions.name: yoyo.migrations.get_migration_hash
documentation:
Return a unique hash given a migration_id, that can be used as a database
key.

```
param migration_id

a migration id (ie filename without extension), or
None if this is a new migration
```

functions.name: yoyo.migrations.read_migrations
documentation: Return a `MigrationList` containing all migrations from `sources`.

functions.name: yoyo.migrations.ancestors
documentation:
Return the dependencies for `migration` from `population`.

```
param migration

a  object

param population

a collection of migrations
```

functions.name: yoyo.migrations.descendants
documentation:
Return all descendants of `migration` from `population`.

```
param migration

a  object

param population

a collection of migrations
```

functions.name: yoyo.migrations.heads
documentation: Return the set of migrations that have no child dependencies

classes.name: yoyo.migrations.PostApplyHookMigration
documentation:
A special migration that is run after successfully applying a set of
migrations. Unlike a normal migration this will be run every time
migrations are applied script is called.

classes.name: yoyo.migrations.TransactionWrapper
documentation:
A :class:~`yoyo.migrations.TransactionWrapper` object causes a step to be
run within a single database transaction. Nested transactions are
implemented via savepoints.

classes.name: yoyo.migrations.Transactionless
documentation:
A :class:~`yoyo.migrations.TransactionWrapper` object causes a step to be
run outside of a database transaction.

classes.name: yoyo.migrations.MigrationStep

methods.name: yoyo.migrations.MigrationStep._execute
documentation:
Execute the given statement. If rows are returned, output these in a
tabulated format.

methods.name: yoyo.migrations.MigrationStep.apply
documentation:
Apply the step.

```
param force

If true, errors will be logged but not be re-raised
```

methods.name: yoyo.migrations.MigrationStep.rollback
documentation: Rollback the step.

Model a single migration.

Each migration step comprises apply and rollback steps of up and down SQL
statements.

classes.name: yoyo.migrations.StepGroup
documentation: Multiple steps aggregated together

classes.name: yoyo.migrations.MigrationList
documentation: A list of database migrations.

classes.name: yoyo.migrations.StepCollector

methods.name: yoyo.migrations.StepCollector.add_step
documentation:
Wrap the given apply and rollback code in a transaction, and add it
to the list of steps.
Return the transaction-wrapped step.

methods.name: yoyo.migrations.StepCollector.add_step_group
documentation: Create a `StepGroup` group of steps.

Provide the `step` and `transaction` functions used in migration
scripts.

Each call to step/transaction updates the StepCollector's `steps` list.
